summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte colorspace signature '1CLR' (cmsSig1colorData) at the FOURCC field offset 0x10-0x13, not the common 'RGB '.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The profile colorspace signature field (FOURCC at input offsets 0x10-0x13) must equal the 4-byte literal '1CLR' (31 43 4c 52) so the switch over ProfileSpace dispatches to the case cmsSig1colorData arm; the common 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) FOURCC instead falls to case cmsSigRgbData.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving Seed 1 carries 31 43 4c 52 ('1CLR') at offsets 0x10-0x13, and the byte-diff shows the winning offsets 0x10-0x12 hold non-RGB values (e.g. 31/43/4c) while losers are uniformly R/G/B; value_profile's CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs whose signature operand moves progressively closer to the 1CLR constant, so the matching FOURCC is assembled and the true case-arm is taken (case cmsSig1colorData is hit only in W).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds hold the fixed FOURCC 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13, which dispatches to case cmsSigRgbData; naive has only edge-count feedback and no comparison-distance gradient, so random havoc never assembles the exact 1CLR signature and the blocker case stays unreached (L: T=0 at line 827).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile augments naive's edge counters with CMP_MAP buckets keyed by operand-distance; the switch on the colorspace signature compiles to integer comparisons against the case constants, so value_profile receives a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient guiding the FOURCC field toward the cmsSig1colorData constant, whereas naive (random byte mutation, no CMP introspection) cannot. This is a CMP_MAP gradient effect, not input-to-state substitution (value_profile has no I2S mutator).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a switch case-arm on the colorspace signature requiring the cmsSig1colorData value.
Dispatch is a switch over the profile colorspace signature.
A winner-resolving seed carries the FOURCC '1CLR' (31 43 4c 52) at offsets 0x10-0x13.
Loser-blocking seeds hold the fixed FOURCC 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13.
At the signature offsets W and L bytes differ, with losers uniformly R/G/B.
The winner takes the blocker true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 827  T=3 F=3  T=0 F=62  case cmsSig1colorData:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in all 10.
value_profile feeds new-CMP_MAP-bucket signal so an input closer to matching a CMP operand pair is preserved.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only per-edge hit-count feedback and no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so the mechanism is CMP-distance feedback not I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply; the winner is value_profile which the prompt's MECHANISM CONTEXT states has no I2SRandReplace mutator, and the CMP_MAP bucket feedback is documented in the same section as the operative gradient signal.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating a switch case on an exact 4-byte signature constant where value_profile fails to out-resolve naive (no dose-response as the matched-prefix difficulty varies), indicating the CMP_MAP distance gradient does not drive assembly of the FOURCC.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one of the three winner seeds shows the literal '1CLR' FOURCC; the other two winners hold different signatures, so it is uncertain whether 1CLR specifically or any non-RGB colorData FOURCC is the resolving feature.
